CAD Projects

1 / 4
01
Biomedical Device — CSF Leptin Delivery
DialyCare Leptin Pump
Led development of an implantable pump delivering leptin into cerebrospinal fluid to prevent Type II diabetes. Full parametric CAD assembly in SolidWorks with custom silicone bellow for controlled low-flow drug delivery. Engineered to deliver 0.3 mL/day over 6 months within ±0.1 mL accuracy. Prototyped using 3D-printed ABS and moulded platinum-cured silicone with leak testing and failure analysis.
SolidWorksABS / Silicone PrototypeCSF Drug DeliveryPerfluorodecalinBiocompatible Design
Key Specs
Shell radius 5.2 cm
Catheter 35 cm
Pressure 1.8 kPa
Temp 37°C
Dose/day 0.3 mL
Battery 10 yrs
1 / 1
02
Industrial Automation
5-DOF Robotic Arm
Five-degree-of-freedom articulated robotic arm designed for precision industrial manipulation. Modular joint assemblies, counterbalanced links, and internal cable routing. FEA validated at peak-torque loading with factor-of-safety ≥ 2.5 across all link members.
SolidWorksFEA Analysis5-DOF KinematicsServo Integration6061-T6 Al
Animation
1 / 3
1 / 3
03
Consumer Product Design — Rapid Prototyping
Headrest Phone & Hook Mount
Designed to make the backseat of a car more comfortable and organized. A headrest-mounted holder that keeps phones secure, provides hooks for small items, and clamps directly onto existing headrest posts — no tools required. Used rapid prototyping to refine fit, strength, and usability across multiple iterations.
Fusion 360 FDM 3D Printing Rapid Prototyping Consumer Product Functional Prototype
Components
Headrest hooks 2×
Phone platforms Top & bottom
Long hooks Bag/item storage
Material PLA filament

About

RA
Rayan AlamCAD & Mechanical Design Engineer
Rayan Alam
Engineer & Designer
M.Sc.
Mechanical EngineeringUniversity of California, Los Angeles — Expected Dec 2026
B.Sc.
Biomedical Engineering – Cum LaudeArizona State University — May 2025

Rayan Alam is a mechanical engineering graduate student at UCLA with a Cum Laude undergraduate degree in biomedical engineering from Arizona State University. Currently a Research Assistant at UCLA's Bionics Lab, working on medical and wearable robotics with emphasis on biomechanics and human–machine interaction.

His CAD and prototyping work spans implantable biomedical devices, industrial robotics, and automotive assemblies — from concept through real bench-tested prototypes. Research experience spans UCLA, MGH/Harvard Medical School, and ASU's Weaver Lab.
